channel.title: NPR Topics: News
channel.description: NPR news, audio, and podcasts. Coverage of breaking stories, national and world news, politics, business, science, technology, and extended coverage of major national and world events.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: Opinion: White House 'gamifying' Iran war updates
item.description: The White House has depicted the war in Iran online with videos that weave real life images of missile strikes and destruction with clips from video games, sports clips, and action movies.
item.content:encoded:
The White House has depicted the war in Iran online with videos that weave real life images of missile strikes and destruction with clips from video games, sports clips, and action movies.

(Image credit: White House via Getty Images)
item.dc:creator: Scott Simon

item.title: There's a massive measles vaccine campaign in Mexico. Is the public on board?
item.description: With tens of thousands of suspected cases, the government is aiming for 2.5 million jabs a week. The response has been encouraging — but also worrisome.
item.content:encoded:
With tens of thousands of suspected cases, the government is aiming for 2.5 million jabs a week. The response has been encouraging — but also worrisome.

(Image credit: Gerardo Vieyra/NurPhoto)
item.dc:creator: Katie Silver

item.title: Crossing the line: Emotional abuse in college sports
item.description: Researchers have found that athletes experience emotional abuse more than any other form of harm. Some athletes maintain that this kind of abuse by coaches can cause lasting, even irreparable damage.
item.content:encoded: Researchers have found that athletes experience emotional abuse more than any other form of harm. Some athletes maintain that this kind of abuse by coaches can cause lasting, even irreparable damage.
item.dc:creator: Julia Haney

item.title: Rifts over Iran, but unity for Trump: Takeaways from CPAC 2026
item.description: Members of the MAGA faithful gathered in Texas for the annual Conservative Political Action Conference. While tensions over Iran split some attendees, Trump remained the glue holding them together.
item.content:encoded:
Members of the MAGA faithful gathered in Texas for the annual Conservative Political Action Conference. While tensions over Iran split some attendees, Trump remained the glue holding them together.

(Image credit: Brandon Bell)
item.dc:creator: Elena Moore

item.title: Stuck in a long TSA line? Here are some strategies if you need to rebook your flight
item.description: Extreme TSA lines at airports have left many passengers scrambling to rebook flights missed due to delays. But while airlines say they're helping flyers, they're not obligated to do so.
item.content:encoded:
Extreme TSA lines at airports have left many passengers scrambling to rebook flights missed due to delays. But while airlines say they're helping flyers, they're not obligated to do so.

(Image credit: Michael M. Santiago)
item.dc:creator: Bill Chappell

item.title: 'What if I die first?' Making a plan is key for family caregivers. Here's how
item.description: People who care for an adult child, partner or sibling have to face the reality that their loved may outlive them. Planning ahead is key but it's not easy.
item.content:encoded: People who care for an adult child, partner or sibling have to face the reality that their loved may outlive them. Planning ahead is key but it's not easy.
item.dc:creator: Kat McGowan

item.title: The iconic South African theater that took on apartheid
item.description: South Africa's iconic Market Theatre, born in the darkest days of apartheid and a force for change, is celebrating its 50th anniversary.
item.content:encoded:
South Africa's iconic Market Theatre, born in the darkest days of apartheid and a force for change, is celebrating its 50th anniversary.

(Image credit: Ruphin Coudyzer)
item.dc:creator: Kate Bartlett

item.title: U.S. troops injured in attack on Saudi base as the war reaches one month
item.description: As the war in Iran reaches the one-month mark, a Iranian strike on an air base in Saudi Arabia wounded several U.S. service members. On Saturday the Israeli military intercepted a missile launched from Yemen.
item.content:encoded:
As the war in Iran reaches the one-month mark, a Iranian strike on an air base in Saudi Arabia wounded several U.S. service members. On Saturday the Israeli military intercepted a missile launched from Yemen.

(Image credit: Majid Saeedi)
item.dc:creator: NPR Staff

item.title: Does the U.S. have a chance in the World Cup? 2 important games will provide clues
item.description: The U.S. has gone unbeaten in its past five international matches. But now the team is upping the ante with games against Belgium and Portugal that could show fans whether a deep run is in the cards.
item.content:encoded:
The U.S. has gone unbeaten in its past five international matches. But now the team is upping the ante with games against Belgium and Portugal that could show fans whether a deep run is in the cards.

(Image credit: Russell Lewis)
item.dc:creator: Becky Sullivan

item.title: 'No Kings' aims for record turnout in Saturday's anti-Trump protests
item.description: Organizers behind No Kings, a network of progressive groups, says protesters will stage demonstrations across the country and abroad to speak out against the Trump administration's actions.
item.content:encoded:
Organizers behind No Kings, a network of progressive groups, says protesters will stage demonstrations across the country and abroad to speak out against the Trump administration's actions.

(Image credit: Julia Demaree Nikhinson)
item.dc:creator: Alana Wise
